page: Amazon Web Services m8azn.3xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor. This x86_64 architecture system features 12 dedicated physical cores and 12 vCPUs operating at a high clock speed of 5.0 GHz. It is equipped with 48.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression tests all placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for large blocks are also strong, ranking in the top 25%. For specialized workloads, the processor's support for AVX-512 vector instructions contributes to top-tier performance in image processing, text processing, and small-scale language model inference tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8azn.3xlarge offers high resource density and exceptional single-threaded performance relative to its general-purpose hardware profile. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU accelerators represents a tradeoff for storage-heavy or deep learning training workloads. This instance is highly suited for high-performance web serving, single-threaded data processing, software compilation, and medium-scale database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.3xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance featuring an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor with 12 dedicated physical cores and 12 vCPUs running at 5.0 GHz. It is configured with 48.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% for single-threaded tasks, and strong large-block memory bandwidth. The instance is highly efficient for workloads requiring high clock speeds and balanced memory allocation without the need for local NVMe drives. It is well-suited for high-performance web serving, text and image processing, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.3xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring a 12-core AMD EPYC processor at 5.0 GHz, 48 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier single-core performance.

tagline: A high-frequency AMD EPYC general-purpose instance delivering top-tier single-core performance and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding compute workloads.

48.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS connectivity
Best suited for high-performance web serving, text processing, and small LLM inference
